name: 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates
description: 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are sample-ready media plates used to detect and quantify coliforms in drinking water samples.

taxonomy.sub_category: 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates Updated on January 24, 2024 · Created on October 2, 2019 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are sample-ready media plates used to detect and quantify coliforms in drinking water samples. Developed By 3M Tested By AOAC Research Institute International Organization for Standardization (ISO) Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are sample-ready culture medium systems that can detect and measure coliform levels in water or beverages within 24 hours. This product is not documented for use in industries other than bottled water, which excludes testing of surface and municipal waters, or waters used in the pharmaceutical or cosmetic industries. 3M™ Petriﬁlm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are manufactured and distributed by 3M™, an American multinational conglomerate company. This product comes in two presentations: 50 plates/box or 1000 plates/case. The 50 plates/box are: 59.91 USD (Thomas Scientific Inc.), 85.00 USD (spreader included) (Nelson James). The 1000 plates/case are: 902.41 USD (Thomas Scientific Inc.), 690.00 USD spreader included (Nelson James) and 989.90 USD (892,00 €) (France VWR) Converted on August 16 2019 Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $989.90 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Small and Medium-sized Enterprises, Public Sector Agencies Distributors / Implementing Organizations 3M™ Petri?lm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates can be purchased through 3M™ food safety U.S. authorized distributors. Countries United States Manufacturing/Building Method 3M™ Petri?lm™ Aqua Coliform Count Plates are mass produced by 3M™ , an American company based in Maplewood, Minnesota, USA. This product is manufactured at Brookings, SD, USA. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Users can purchase these products through 3M™ food safety U.S. authorized distributors. Distributions to Date Status Unknown Test types included Coliforms Equipment included Freezer, incubator, standard colony counter or other illuminated magnifier Tests per kit 50 tests per box or 1000 tests per case Detection range Unknown Processing time for one sample (hr) 24 hours Additional materials required Pipettes, 3M™ Petrifilm™ Spreader Power required (yes/no) Yes Complete kit weight (kg) Unknown Design Specifications The 3M™ Petrifilm™ Aqua Coliform Count (AQCC) Plates comes in two presentations: 1000 plates/case or 50 plates/box. These sample-ready media plates contain Violet Red Bile (VRB) nutrients, a cold-water-soluble gelling agent, and an indicator dye (tetrazolium) which colors all colonies red. The top film traps the gases produced by the coliforms, indicating confirmed colonies. For more information see the Interpretation guide. For specific instructions on how to use the 3M™ Petrifilm™ Aqua Coliform Count (AQCC) Plates see research and standards. This product is composed of the following ingredients: adhesive, guar gum, polypropylene film, hinge tape, lactose, and pancreatic digest of gelatin. Product Schematics Technical Support Users are responsible for familiarizing themselves with product instructions and information. They can contact 3M™ product support for specific product and application questions. Replacement Components Single use plates Lifecycle 3M™ Petrifilm™ Aqua Coliform Count (AQCC) Plates must be stored at temperatures less than or equal to 8ºC and have a shelf life of 18 months from date of manufacture. Expiration and lot number are indicated on each package. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters 3M™ Petrifilm™ Plates were designed to be ready to use, to reduce or eliminate time-consuming agar prep, to provide consistent, reliable results, and to have a compact size using less storage/incubator space. The system is also designed to be more sustainable in comparison to traditional agar methods, by using less energy and water, and produce less waste therefore reducing the products carbon footprint. Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety This product is considered to be an article which does not release or otherwise result in exposure to a hazardous chemical under normal use conditions. This product is exempt from hazard classification according to OSHA Hazard Communication Standard, 29 CFR 1910.1200. Complementary Technical Systems This product requires a 3M™ Petrifilm™ Spreader to extend the inoculum over uniform area. A standard colony counter or other illuminated magnifier is required to count colonies.
